item.title: Odd Lots Is Coming to London
item.description: Our first live show in the UK.

item.title: Canadians Back Gas Tax Cuts as Top Response to Oil Price Spike, Poll Finds
item.description: Canadians rank cutting fuel taxes as the No. 1 action Prime Minister Mark Carney should take to offset higher gasoline prices.

item.title: IMF Chief Says Oil Shock Tests a World With Little Fiscal Buffer
item.description: The International Monetary Fund said that the conflict in the Middle East is a major supply shock that will test the resilience of a world with limited scope for fiscal support, even as US and Iran have negotiated a two-week ceasefire.

item.title: CVC Said to Seek Partners to Back €10.9 Billion Recordati Deal
item.description: CVC Capital Partners Plc is seeking co-investors to help bankroll its proposed €10.9 billion ($12.7 billion) takeover of Italian drugmaker Recordati SpA, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Bank of America Sees a $1.1 Trillion Market for Sports Event Betting in US
item.description: Bank of America Corp. estimates the potential market for US sports-related event contracts at roughly $1.1 trillion in annual volume, as prediction markets boom anew.

item.title: How Starlink Is Helping Iranians Evade Wartime Internet Blackout
item.description: The near-total shutdown of the internet in Iran is the world’s longest nationwide blackout since the Arab Spring in the early 2010s.

item.title: Treasuries Steady After Mixed Economic Data as Oil Rebounds
item.description: US Treasuries held steady after the Federal Reserve’s preferred inflation gauge showed elevated price pressures even before the US attack on Iran sent energy costs sharply higher.

item.title: Former DeepMind Researchers Bet on Visual AI With New Startup
item.description: Former Google DeepMind researcher Andrew Dai believes that the artificial intelligence models at big labs have the intelligence of a 3-year-old kid, at least when it comes to making sense of visual prompts.

item.title: Paramount M&A Loan Cut to $49 Billion as Lending Group Swells
item.description: Bank of America Corp. and Citigroup Inc. have sold down a multi-billion dollar bridge loan backing Paramount Skydance Corp.’s takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. to a wider group of banks, a key step in one of the biggest M&A financings of the year.

item.title: Intel Wins Google Commitment to Use Xeon Chips in Data Centers
item.description: Intel Corp., trying to promote the use of its technology in data centers, said Alphabet Inc.’s Google has committed to using future generations of its Xeon processors and other chips.

item.title: SiFive to Fuel Data Center Push With $400 Million Funding Round
item.description: Chip startup SiFive Inc. received $400 million in funding from a group of investors led by Atreides Management, money the company plans to use to get a bigger foothold in AI data centers.

item.title: Rates Rout Fuels Worst Month for Brazil Hedge Funds Since 2020
item.description: Brazilian hedge funds saw their worst month in six years in March as a surge in oil prices upended bets on lower interest rates globally, a popular trade among the country’s money managers.

item.title: CoreWeave Tapping Junk Debt Market After Meta AI Computing Deal
item.description: CoreWeave Inc. is tapping the junk bond market after expanding a deal to supply artificial intelligence computing power to Meta Platforms Inc.

item.title: Czech Republic Seeks to Extend Dukovany Reactor Life by 20 Years
item.description: The Czech Republic is seeking to extend the operating life of the Dukovany nuclear power station into the mid-2060s after safety analysis indicated the plant can run longer than originally planned.

item.title: Dollar Moves in Lockstep With Oil Prices After Iran War Surge
item.description: The dollar and oil are trading in lockstep in the wake of the US-Iran ceasefire this week, tightening the relationship between them to near-record levels.

item.title: A 1,300% Rally Turns a Tiny Shipping ETF Into an Iran War Gauge
item.description: Every twist in the Iran conflict — every ceasefire bet, every missile strike, every shift in tanker traffic — shows up almost instantly in a $65 million exchange-traded fund that most investors have never heard of.

item.title: US Recurring Jobless Claims Fall to Almost Two-Year Low
item.description: Recurring applications for US jobless benefits fell to the lowest level in almost two years, adding to evidence of stabilization in the labor market.

item.title: US Consumer Spending Barely Rose, Inflation Lingered Pre-War
item.description: US consumer spending barely rose in February against a backdrop of persistent inflation that’s set to accelerate due to the Iran war.

item.title: US Stock Futures Slip as Ceasefire Doubts Cool Relief Rally
item.description: US equity futures slipped on Thursday, paring the previous session’s sharp rally as skepticism over the US-Iran ceasefire crept back into markets and geopolitical risks remained elevated.

item.title: Mexico Inflation Rose Slightly Less Than Expected in March
item.description: Mexico’s annual inflation accelerated last month after the central bank resumed its easing cycle and marked up inflation forecasts with the Iran war pressuring energy costs globally.

item.title: Poland Keeps Rates on Hold to Gauge Iran War Impact on Inflation
item.description: The Polish central bank left interest rates unchanged on Thursday as policymakers try to assess the impact of the Iran war on inflation following the announcement of a fragile truce in the fighting.

item.title: Marsh’s Mercer Raises $3.8 Billion for Private Investments
item.description: Marsh & McLennan Cos’ Mercer has raised just over $3.8 billion for private investments.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: CoreWeave, Instacart, Texas Instruments
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 0.3% as of 7:55 a.m. in New York as optimism about the ceasefire in the Middle East wavers.

item.title: Sleijpen Says ECB Will Act If Needed to Keep Inflation at Target
item.description: The European Central Bank is prepared to respond if the Iran war drives inflation away from its 2% target, according to Governing Council member Olaf Sleijpen.

item.title: Samsung to Invest $4 Billion in Chip Packaging Site in Vietnam
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co. plans a $4 billion outlay to build a chip packaging plant in northern Vietnam, as the country’s largest foreign investor expands its footprint in the country.

item.title: Earnings Optimism Enhances Stocks’ Allure as Iran Worries Ease
item.description: Investors that slashed their stock exposure as war flared in the Middle East may soon have a fresh reason for piling back into equities: the upcoming earnings season.

item.title: Philippines Says China Fired Flares in Airspace ‘Bullying’
item.description: The Philippines said Chinese forces fired flares directly at its coast guard aircraft over the Spratly Islands in the disputed South China Sea, calling the incident “a clear and deliberate act of bullying.”

item.title: Iran-War Pause Opens Door for Congo’s Maiden Eurobond Sale
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo is rushing to sell its maiden dollar bonds, taking advantage of a ceasefire in the war between the US and Iran.

item.title: Oil Chaos Is Clearest in the Hunt for ASAP Barrels
item.description: With the market suffering a massive loss of supply, refiners are scouting the world for crude.

item.title: Coreweave Expands Meta Deal for AI Computing to $21 Billion
item.description: CoreWeave Inc. has expanded its deal to supply artificial intelligence computing power to Meta Platforms Inc. to $21 billion, building on a previous $14.2 billion agreement the companies struck in September.
